nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A mutual exclusion algorithm for N processes where at most one process is in the critical section at a time.

Hints: Should define processes as 1..N, track state as {idle, trying, critical}.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE MutualExclusion ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N   (* number of processes *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Variables -----------------------------------------------------------

VARIABLES procState    (* mapping from each process id to its current state *), procState

vars == <<procState>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* State definitions -----------------------------------------------

Idle      == "idle"
Trying    == "trying"
Critical  == "critical"

StatesSet == { Idle , Trying , Critical }

ProcessIds == DOMAIN [i |-> i IN 1 .. N]

TypeOk ==
     /\ Domain(procState) = ProcessIds
     /\  \A  p : p \in ProcessIds => 
          procState[p] \in StatesSet

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Initial condition ----------------------------------------------

Init ==
     TypeOk /\
     (\E c \in ProcessIds :
        (/\ procState[c] #= Critical
         /\ UNCHANGED <<(UNUSED)>>))

(* The initial assignment ensures that no process starts already in the critical section.  
   All other states are unconstrained but must be one of the allowed values.
*)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Next-state relation ---------------------------------------------

Next ==
     \/ TRYING_TO_CRITICAL
     \/ CRIT_EXITED
     \/ Terminating

TRYING_TO_CRITICAL ==
       LET P == CHOOSE p WHERE procState[p] = Idle
           Qs == {q: q \in ProcessIds & q /= P}
       IN
            /\ procState'[P] = Trying
            /\ UnchangedExcept(P)


                            ((o <> P) /\ (procState[o] = Critical))
       IN
            /\ !OthersInCrit  (* ensure at most one can enter *)
            /\ procState'[P] = Critical
            /\ UnchangedExcept(P)

CRIT_EXITED ==
      LET C == CHOICE p : procState[p] = Critical 
          InTryOther != FALSE
              ==  \E  t \in ProcessIds :
                    ((t #= C)
                     /\\ procState[t] = Trying )
      IN
        /\ UNCHANGED <<(UNUSED)>>
        /\ IF InTryOther THEN
             /* If another process is trying, it may proceed to critical */
             TRUE   (* placeholder for potential transition actual logic omitted *)

             /* No other processes are in the "trying" state – release CS */
             procState'[C] = Idle

Terminating ==
     /\ UNCHANGED vars

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

====

---- MODULE MutualExclusion ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets
CONSTANTS N

VARIABLES state, flag, turn

(* State of each process: idle, trying, critical *)
State == {"idle", "trying", "critical"}

(* Initial state: all processes idle, no flags set, turn arbitrary *)
Init == /\ state \in [1..N -> State]
        /\ flag \in [1..N -> BOOLEAN]
        /\ turn \in 1..N
        /\ \A i \in 1..N: state[i] = "idle" /\ flag[i] = FALSE

(* Next-state relation: process i can move from idle to trying, from trying to critical if turn = i, from critical to idle *)
Next == \E i \in 1..N:
          \/ /\ state[i] = "idle"
             /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "trying"]
             /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![i] = TRUE]
             /\ turn' = turn
          \/ /\ state[i] = "trying"
             /\ turn = i
             /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "critical"]
             /\ flag' = flag
             /\ turn' = turn
          \/ /\ state[i] = "critical"
             /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "idle"]
             /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![i] = FALSE]
             /\ turn' = turn

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, flag, turn>>

(* Mutual exclusion: no two processes in critical section simultaneously *)
TypeOK == \A i, j \in 1..N: i # j => ~(state[i] = "critical" /\ state[j] = "critical")

====

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A two-phase commit protocol with one coordinator and N participants. The coordinator decides to commit only if all participants vote yes.

Hints: Messages: Prepare, VoteYes, VoteNo, Commit, Abort.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE TwoPhaseCommit ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT Participants
\* Assume each identifier is from {1 .. P} for some constant P
CONSTDEF 
    ParticipantSet == [p \in Participants |-> p]

VARIABLES 
    phase          ,\\ Current phase of the transaction ("init","prepare","commit","abort"), messages, msgs, votes
    votes         ,\\ Mapping from participants to their last received message type or NONE
    messages      ,\\ Multiset of pending messages in transit elements are tuples <sender, receiver, msgType>

(* Message types *)
MessageTypes == {"Prepare", "VoteYes", "VoteNo", "Commit", "Abort"}

(* Helper constants *)
NONE  == <<>> 

(********************************************************************)
*** Type invariant (ensures variables stay within finite domains) ***
(********************************************************************)

TypeOK ==
    /\ phase \in {"init", "prepare", "commit", "abort"}
    /\ votes \in SUBSET[Participants -> MessageTypes]
       (* Each entry maps a participant to one of the defined message types *)
    /\ UNCHANGED @votes[*] \/ (\A v : Votes[v]) #v = NONE => FALSE
    /\ messages \subseteq [{ sender: ANY,
                             receiver: ANY,
                             kind: MessageTypes }]

(********************************************************************
 *** Initial state specification                                 *****
 *******************************************************************)  

Init ==
    /\ phase   = "init"
    /\ votes   = {}
    /\ messages =
        {<"Coordinator", p, "Prepare"> | p \in Participants}

(*********************************************************************
 ***** Next-state relation definition                               *
 * All disjuncts must specify ALL vars either by prime assignment 
 * or using UNCHANGED.                                            *
 *********************************************************************)

Next ==
    LET
      sendMsg(s,r,m) == < s , r , m >

      deliver(msg) ==
          /\ msg.kind \in MessageTypes
          /\ IF msg.receiver = "Coordinator" THEN
                CASE msg.sender \in Participants ->
                    /\ msgs'  = [msgKind IN MessageTypes |
                                  (IF msgKind = "VoteYes" OR msgKind="VoteNo") =>
                                      IF EXISTS x \in participants :
                                         ((x,msgKind)=msg)
                                     THEN [msgs EXCEPT ![msgKind]=TRUE]

                   (* Coordinator updates its vote record *)
              /\\ UNCHANGED <<phase,votes>>
            [] TRUE -> UNCHANGED <<phase,votes>>  

          \/ IF msg.receiver \in Participants THEN
                 /\ IF msg.kind ="Commit" THEN
                        /* Participant commits */
                     /\ UNCHANGED <<votes,messages>>
                  /\ phase'=phase
               /\ votes'=votes
             /\ messages'=UNION(messages,\{sendMsg(msg.receiver,"Coordinator","Ack"\})\}


        in
     /\ (\E p : p \in Participants & NOT p \in DOMAIN(votes)) 
         /\ messageToSend? := {<p,"Coordinator",MessageTypes> | ...}   \\ placeholder for sending Vote requests?

(*********************************************************************
 ***** Termination condition to avoid deadlock                      *
 *********************************************************************)  

Terminating ==
    /\ phase = "commit"
      \/ phase = "abort"

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<phase,messages>>

====

The dining philosophers problem with N philosophers and N forks. Each philosopher picks up both adjacent forks to eat, then puts them down.

Hints: Track fork ownership and philosopher states: thinking, hungry, eating.

\* States are represented by integers:

---- MODULE DiningPhilosophers ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N

(* Define philosophers and forks as the set {1, 2, ..., N} *)
Philosophers == 1..N
Forks == 1..N

(* Helper functions to get left and right forks for a philosopher *)
LeftFork(i) == IF i = 1 THEN N ELSE i - 1
RightFork(i) == IF i = N THEN 1 ELSE i + 1

(* State variables *)
VARIABLES state, forks

(* State can be "thinking", "hungry", or "eating" for each philosopher *)
State == {"thinking", "hungry", "eating"}

(* Initial state: all philosophers are thinking and all forks are free *)
Init ==
    /\ state = [p \in Philosophers |-> "thinking"]
    /\ forks = [f \in Forks |-> TRUE]

(* Helper predicate: a philosopher can start eating if both adjacent forks are free *)
CanEat(i) == forks[LeftFork(i)] /\ forks[RightFork(i)]

(* Action: philosopher i picks up both forks and starts eating *)
TakeForks(i) ==
    /\ state[i] = "hungry"
    /\ CanEat(i)
    /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "eating"]
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![LeftFork(i)] = FALSE, ![RightFork(i)] = FALSE]

(* Action: philosopher i puts down both forks and goes back to thinking *)
PutForks(i) ==
    /\ state[i] = "eating"
    /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "thinking"]
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![LeftFork(i)] = TRUE, ![RightFork(i)] = TRUE]

(* Action: philosopher i becomes hungry *)
Hungry(i) ==
    /\ state[i] = "thinking"
    /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "hungry"]
    /\ forks' = forks

(* Next-state relation: any philosopher can transition to the next state *)
Next ==
    \E i \in Philosophers :
        TakeForks(i) \/ PutForks(i) \/ Hungry(i)

(* Invariant: no two adjacent philosophers are eating simultaneously *)
NoAdjacentEating ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        ~(state[i] = "eating" /\ state[IF i = N THEN 1 ELSE i + 1] = "eating")

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only eat if both adjacent forks are free *)
ForksFreeWhenEating ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "eating" => forks[LeftFork(i)] /\ forks[RightFork(i)]

(* Invariant: a fork is held by at most one philosopher *)
ForksHeldByAtMostOne ==
    \A f \in Forks :
        (\A i, j \in Philosophers :
            (f = LeftFork(i) /\ f = LeftFork(j)) => i = j)

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only pick up a fork if it is free *)
ForksFreeBeforePickingUp ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "hungry" => forks[LeftFork(i)] /\ forks[RightFork(i)]

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only put down a fork if it is holding it *)
ForksHeldBeforePuttingDown ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "eating" => ~forks[LeftFork(i)] /\ ~forks[RightFork(i)]

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only become hungry if it is not already hungry *)
NotAlreadyHungry ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "thinking" => ~state[i] = "hungry"

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only become hungry if it is not already eating *)
NotAlreadyEating ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "thinking" => ~state[i] = "eating"

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only become hungry if it is not already thinking *)
NotAlreadyThinking ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "thinking" => ~state[i] = "thinking"

(* Specification *)
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, forks>>

(* Type invariant *)
TypeOK ==
    /\ state \in [Philosophers -> State]
    /\ forks \in [Forks -> BOOLEAN]

====

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Lamport's bakery mutual exclusion algorithm for N processes. Processes take a numbered ticket; lower numbers enter first.

Hints: VARIABLES num, flag. Choosing phase before assigning number.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE Bakery ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT n   (* Number of processes *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Variables:
\*    num[p] : current ticket number held by process p (or 0 when not choosing)
\*    flag[p]: boolean indicating whether process p is currently selecting a ticket
VARIABLES num, flag

vars == <<num, flag>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
TypeOK ==
        /\ num \in [1 .. n -> Nat]
        /\ flag \in [1 .. n -> BOOLEAN]

Init ==
        /\ num = [p \in 1 .. n |-> 0]
        /\ flag = [p \in 1 .. n |-> FALSE]

Choose(p) ==
        LET maxNum == MAX(num[1..n])
            newTicket == IF maxNum # 0 THEN maxNum + 1 ELSE 1 IN
          /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![p]'=TRUE]
          /\ UNCHANGED[num]
        \/ /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![p]'=FALSE]
           /\ num'[p] = newTicket
           /\ UNCHANGED[[q \in 1 .. n |-> q /= p][num[q]]]

EnterCriticalSection(p) ==
        /\ flag[p] = TRUE
        /\ FORALL r \in 1 .. n :
                (\(r#p => 
                    ((num[r]=0) \/  
                     (IF num[r]<num[p] THEN TRUE

                      (* If equal numbers then lower id wins *) 
                       THEN r < p)))
               )
        /\ UNCHANGED <<flag, num>>

ExitProcess(p) ==
        /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![p]='\*']
        /\ UNCHANGED[<<flag>>]

Next ==
     \E  p \in 1 .. n : Choose(p)
   \/  \A  p \in 1 .. n : EnterCriticalSection(p)
   \/ Terminating

Terminating == /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ [] Next /\\ TypeOK

====

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A bounded FIFO queue with one producer and one consumer. The producer blocks when the queue is full; the consumer blocks when empty.

Hints: Queue capacity is a constant K.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE BoundedFIFO ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences

CONSTANT K   (* Capacity of the queue *)

VARIABLE q      (* Sequence representing contents *), head, item, q, size, tail
          head    (* Index for next dequeue position *)
          tail    (* Index for next enqueue position *)
          size    (* Current number of elements *)

vars == <<q, head, tail, size>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
TypeOk ==
    /\ q \subseteq Nat            (* Elements are natural numbers *)
       /\ Len(q) = size           (* Size matches sequence length *)
       /\ head \in 1 .. K + 1     (* Circular indices in [1,K+1] *)
       /\ tail \in 1 .. K + 1
       /\ size \in 0 .. K         (* Number of items within bounds *)

Init ==
    /\ q = []                     (* Empty queue initially *)
    /\ head = 1                   (* Start at index 1 *)
    /\ tail = 2                    (* Tail points to first free slot (index 2) *)
    /\ size = 0

ProducerAction(item) ==
    LET newTail == IF tail # K THEN tail + 1 ELSE 1 IN
        /\ size < K                 (* Queue not full *)
        /\ item' \notin q             (* Ensure distinctness if desired optional *)
        /\ q' = Append(SeqSubseq(q, 1..size), SeqFromList([item]))

---- MODULE ReadWriteLock ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N \* Number of readers

VARIABLES readers, writer_active

(* readers is a set of reader IDs currently holding the lock *)
(* writer_active is a boolean: 1 if a writer holds the lock, 0 otherwise *)

        /\ writer_active = 0

(* Reader i attempts to acquire the lock *)
ReaderAcquire(i) == 
    /\ i \in 1..N
    /\ writer_active = 0
    /\ readers' = readers \cup {i}
    /\ UNCHANGED writer_active

(* Reader i releases the lock *)
ReaderRelease(i) == 
    /\ i \in readers
    /\ readers' = readers \ {i}
    /\ UNCHANGED writer_active

(* Writer attempts to acquire the lock *)
WriterAcquire == 
    /\ writer_active = 0
    /\ readers = {}
    /\ writer_active' = 1
    /\ UNCHANGED readers

(* Writer releases the lock *)
WriterRelease == 
    /\ writer_active = 1
    /\ writer_active' = 0
    /\ UNCHANGED readers

Next == \/ \E i \in 1..N: ReaderAcquire(i)
        \/ \E i \in readers: ReaderRelease(i)
        \/ WriterAcquire
        \/ WriterRelease

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<readers, writer_active>>

(* Invariants *)
TypeOK == /\ readers \subseteq 1..N
          /\ writer_active \in {0,1}
          /\ (writer_active = 1 => readers = {})

NoConcurrentWriters == \A i, j \in 1..N: ~(i # j /\ i \in readers /\ j \in readers)

NoReaderDuringWrite == \A i \in 1..N: ~(writer_active = 1 /\ i \in readers)

---- MODULE ChandyLamport ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences

CONSTANT N
ASSUME N \in 1..10

VARIABLES state, channel, marker

(* Type constraints *)
TypeOK == /\ state \in [1..N -> {"active", "recorded"}]
          /\ channel \in [1..N -> [1..N -> {"empty", "msg"}]]
          /\ marker \in [1..N -> [1..N -> BOOLEAN]]

(* Initial state: all processes active, channels empty, no markers *)
Init == /\ state = [i \in 1..N |-> "active"]
        /\ channel = [i \in 1..N |-> [j \in 1..N |-> "empty"]]
        /\ marker = [i \in 1..N |-> [j \in 1..N |-> FALSE]]

(* Process i records its state and sends markers on all outgoing channels *)
Record(i) == /\ state[i] = "recorded"
             /\ \A j \in 1..N : marker[i][j] = TRUE

(* Sending a marker on channel i->j *)
SendMarker(i, j) == /\ channel[i][j] = "empty"
                   /\ channel' = [channel EXCEPT ![i][j] = "msg"]
                   /\ marker' = [marker EXCEPT ![i][j] = FALSE]
                   /\ UNCHANGED <<state>>

(* Receiving a marker on channel j->i *)
ReceiveMarker(j, i) == /\ channel[j][i] = "msg"
                      /\ channel' = [channel EXCEPT ![j][i] = "empty"]
                      /\ marker' = [marker EXCEPT ![j][i] = FALSE]
                      /\ UNCHANGED <<state>>

(* Sending a normal message on channel i->j *)
SendMsg(i, j) == /\ channel[i][j] = "empty"
                 /\ channel' = [channel EXCEPT ![i][j] = "msg"]
                 /\ UNCHANGED <<state, marker>>

(* Receiving a normal message on channel j->i *)
ReceiveMsg(j, i) == /\ channel[j][i] = "msg"
                   /\ channel' = [channel EXCEPT ![j][i] = "empty"]
                   /\ UNCHANGED <<state, marker>>

(* Next-state relation *)
Next == \E i \in 1..N :
          \E j \in 1..N :
            (state[i] = "active" /\ Record(i) /\ SendMarker(i, j))
            \/ (marker[j][i] /\ ReceiveMarker(j, i))
            \/ (SendMsg(i, j))
            \/ (ReceiveMsg(j, i))

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, channel, marker>>

Hints: Model message loss as non-deterministic. Track retransmit count.

---- MODULE FileTransfer ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT CHUNK_SIZE, FILE_LENGTH, PACKET_ID, PACKET_IDS

    /* Maximum number of retries for each chunk */
    MAX_RETRY     >= 1,

    /* Set of all possible packet identifiers (chunk indices) */
    PACKET_IDS      : SUBSET Nat,

    /* Channel capacity is unlimited but we model it explicitly */
    CHANNEL_CAPACITY : Nat 

/* Derived constants */
VARIABLES 
    sentChunks ,          (* Sequence of packets already transmitted *), ackedPackets, channelBuffer, currentChunkIndex, retryCount
    ackedPackets ,        (* Subset of PACKET_IDS acknowledged by receiver *)
    retryCount ,         (* Mapping from PACKET_ID -> Int representing remaining attempts *)
    currentChunkIndex ,  (* Index into sequence of data being sent *)
    channelBuffer        (* Queue modeling the unreliably transmitting channel *)

(* Type constraints and invariants *)
TypeOK == /\ sentChunks \in Seq(PACKET_IDS)
           /\ ackedPackets \subseteq PACKET_IDS
           /\ UNCHANGED <<sentChunks>>
           /\ forall p \in PACKET_IDS:
                (\E r: r = retryCount[p] \/ r # retryCount[p])  
               /\ retryCount[p] \in 0..MAX_RETRY + 1
           /\ currentChunkIndex \in 0 .. Len(sentChunks)+CHANNEL_CAPACITY
           /\ channelBuffer \in Seq(Seq(Nat))

Init ==
/* Initially no chunks are in flight, nothing has been received,
   each chunk can be retried MAX_RETRY times. The sender starts with first packet index zero.
*/
\land sentChunks = []
\land ackedPackets = {}
\and retryCount = [p |-> (IF p <= FILE_LENGTH THEN MAX_RETRY ELSE -1)]
\land currentChunkIndex = 0 
\land channelBuffer = []

SendPacket(p) ==
/\ p \notin sentChunks
\/ p > LEN(channelBuffer)
/\
channelBuffer' = Append(channelBuffer , [[p]])
retryCount'[p] = MAX_RETRY

AckReceived(p) ==
/*
 * Non-deterministic message loss is modeled by allowing the receiver to either deliver or drop an ACK.
 */
UNLESS p \# IN CHANNEL_BUFFER then skip else (
    /* Receiver acknowledges and removes from buffer if present */
    UNCHANGED <<sentChunks>>
    /\ ackedPackets' = Insert(ackedPackets,p)
    /\ channelBuffer' = RemoveFirstWhere(\x: x[1]=p)(channelBuffer)

)

/*\* Retransmission logic for a single packet **/
RetransmitIfNeeded(p) ==  
/* If we have not yet exceeded retries, send again otherwise give up on this packet */   
((retryCount[p]>0) => SendPacket(p))
 \/ ((retryCount[p]<=0) =>
     (* Give up – no further action needed. *)
      UnchangedVars )

UnchangedVars == /\ sentChunks'=sentChunks
                 /\ retryCount'=retryCount
                 /\ channelBuffer'=channelBuffer

Next ==
(* The sender can choose one of several actions:
   1. Transmit next chunk (if any remain).
   2. Process acknowledgment received in channel buffer.
   3. Retry sending packets that are still pending but lost.
*)
\/ 
/\ currentChunkIndex < FILE_LENGTH          // there remains data to be transmitted
/\
SendPacket(currentChunkIndex+1)
/\ currentChunkIndex' = currentChunkIndex + 1

\\/ 
/\ Len(channelBuffer)>0                      /* There is at least an ACK waiting */
\\ AckReceived([first |-> first][currentChannel] ) 

\\/ 
FORALL p \in PACKET_IDS :
    (\E r : r=retryCount[p]) -> (
        IF retryCount[p]>0 THEN RetransmitIfNeeded(p)

       )

/* Termination: if all chunks have been acknowledged or given up, we stop transmitting */  
Terminating ==
UNLESS ackedPackets # PACKET_IDS \/ EXISTS p:\(p <=FILE_LENGTH) & retryCount[p]<=-1 then skip else UnchangedVars  

Spec == Init /\\ [][ Next ]_<< sentChunks ,ackdPackets ,retryCount ,
                               currentChunkIndex ,channelBuffer >> /\ TypeOK

          /\ ack \in 0..N
          /\ retries \in 0..MAX_RETRIES
          /\ state \in {"idle", "sending", "waiting_ack", "done", "failed"}

Init == /\ seq = 0
        /\ ack = 0
        /\ retries = 0
        /\ state = "idle"

SendChunk == /\ state = "idle"
             /\ seq < N
             /\ state' = "sending"

Transmit == /\ state = "sending"
            /\ state' = "waiting_ack"

AckReceived == /\ state = "waiting_ack"
               /\ ack = seq
               /\ state' = "idle"
               /\ seq' = seq + 1
               /\ retries' = 0

AckLost == /\ state = "waiting_ack"
           /\ ack # seq
           /\ state' = "waiting_ack"
           /\ retries' = retries + 1
           /\ retries' <= MAX_RETRIES

GiveUp == /\ state = "waiting_ack"
          /\ retries = MAX_RETRIES
          /\ state' = "failed"

Done == /\ seq = N
        /\ state' = "done"

Next == \E s \in {"idle", "sending", "waiting_ack", "done", "failed"}:
          /\ state' = s
          /\ UNCHANGED <<seq, ack, retries>>

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<seq, ack, retries, state>>

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets, TLC, Integers

(* Types *)

TypeDBKey      == [key : DBKeys]
TypeValue       == Nat        (* For simplicity values are natural numbers *)
TypeVersion     == Nat         (* Version number for each key in the database *)

\* Database is mapping from Key -> Value and version
dbRecord(key) ==
    <<value |-> dbState[key].val,
      ver   |-> dbState[key].ver>>

TxId           == Nat            (* Transaction identifiers *)
TxStatus       == {“running”, “committed”, “aborted”}
ReadSetEntry   == [txid  : TxId ,
                   key   : DBKeys ,
                   val   : TypeValue ,
                   ver   : TypeVersion ]
WriteSetEntry  == [txid  : TxId ,
                    key   : DBKeys ,
                    newVal: TypeValue ]

TransactionRec =
[ id      : TxId 
  status  : TxStatus 
  readset : Seq(ReadSetEntry)
  writeset: Seq(WriteSetEntry)
]

InitDb(dbVals) ==
    /\ \A k \in DBKeys :
        (k \mapsto <<val|->dbVals[k], 
                     ver|->[0]>>) \subseteq dbState

InitTx(txsList, nextID) ==
    txs = tlist2seq(txsList)

(* Helper to convert a set of transactions into sequence preserving order by ID *)
tlist2seq(ts) ==

         LET minT == CHOOSE tid IN DOMAIN ts:
                      MIN (\{tid' \mid tid'\in DOMAIN ts\})
          IN <<<ts[minT]]> , tlist2seq(\{i | i in DOMAIN ts & i /= minT})>


TypeOK ==
    /\ dbState \in SUBSET(DBKeys -> [value: TypeValue,
                                     ver  : TypeVersion])
    /\ txs     \in SEQ(TransactionRec)

Next ==
    \/ BeginStep
    \/ ReadStep
    \/ WriteStep
    \/ CommitAttempt
    \/ AbortStep
    \/ Terminating

BeginStep ==
    /* Start new transaction with unique id */
    let maxId == MAX({tx.id | tx <- txs} \cup {1-1})   (* if no tx yet use -1? but we need >=0 so start at 0*)
        newId == (IF maxId=-1 THEN 0 ELSE maxId+1)
      IN 
           /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState, txs>>
           /\ txs = Append(txs, 
               <<id->newId ,
                 status->"running",
                 readset->[],
                 writeset->[ ]>> )

ReadStep ==
    LET txn == CHOOSE t \IN txs :
                t.status="running"
            keyToRead == CHOICE k \in DBKeys:
                            TRUE          (* nondeterministically pick a key *)
         IN

             /\ txs' =
                  [t in DOMAIN(txns):

                          <| txn |
                             readset -> Append(

                                 <<txid->txn.id ,key->keyToRead,
                                   val->dbState[keyToRead].val,
                                   ver->dbState[keyToRead].ver>>)|
                         |>


WriteStep ==
    /* Transaction adds an entry to its write set */
    let txn == CHOOSE t\IN txs : t.status="running"
        targetKey   == CHOICE k \in DBKeys:TRUE
        newValue     == CHOICE v \in 0..10:TRUE      (* bounded value space for simplicity *)
       IN 
           /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState>>
           /\ txs = [
               i \in DOMAIN(txs):  

                     <| txn |
                        writeset ->
                           Append( txn.writeset ,
                               <<txid->txn.id,key->targetKey,newVal->newValue>> ) |>   


CommitAttempt ==
    LET committingTx == CHOOSE t \IN txs :
                            t.status ="running"         

         conflict? == EXISTS w1,w2 \in SEQ(committingTx.writeset) :
                      (w1.key=w2.key & w1.txid /= w2.txid)

         -- For each key in the transaction's readset, check if version changed since snapshot.
         stale?(k,v,rVer) ==
             dbState[k].ver > rVer 

         stalenessExists ==
            EXISTS rsEntry \in Seq(committingTx.readset):
                (\E kv,\vr:\exists e\in Seq(dbRecord(rsEntry.key)):
                    FALSE)
              /* placeholder actual logic below */

         validReadSet ==
            ~stale?

        IN
           \/  /\ !conflict?
                  /\ !validReadSet   (* no conflicts and reads are still consistent *)
                  /\ UNCHANGED<<dbState>>
                  /\ txs' = [i \in DOMAIN(txs) |-> IF i=commitingTx.index THEN <| commitingTx | status -> "committeed">>]

           \/  /\ TRUE      (* abort on any failure: write-write or stale read set *)
               /\ txs'= [
                   i \in DOMAIN(txns):

                         <| txn |
                            status->"aborted"|>


AbortStep ==  
    /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState>>   
    /\ txs' =
          [t \IN DOMAIN(txs):
                 IF t.status="running"
                      THEN <| t |status->"aborted">


Terminating ==
     /\ UNCHANGED dbState,txs

Spec == InitDb(DBKeys) /\
       [][][Next]_vars

          /\ UNCHANGEd offsets

---- MODULE Gossip ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N, Nodes

(* Each node is identified by an integer in 1..N *)
NodeSet == 1..N

(* State: for each node, a set of messages it knows *)
VARIABLES known

(* Initial state: no node knows any message *)
Init == known = [i \in NodeSet |-> {}]

(* Helper: a node i chooses a random neighbor j (j != i) *)
ChooseNeighbor(i) == CHOOSE j \in NodeSet : j # i

(* Helper: node i sends all its known messages to neighbor j *)
Send(i, j) == 
  LET msgs == known[i] IN
  /\ known' = [k \in NodeSet |-> IF k = j THEN known[k] \cup msgs ELSE known[k]]

(* One round of gossip: each node picks a neighbor and sends its knowledge *)
Round == 
  \E i \in NodeSet :
    LET j == ChooseNeighbor(i) IN
    Send(i, j)

(* Next-state relation: one round of gossip *)
Next == Round

(* Spec: execution of the protocol *)
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_known

(* Invariant: all known sets are subsets of the universe of messages *)
TypeOK == \A i \in NodeSet : known[i] \subseteq 1..N

---- MODULE MemoryAllocator ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N
Pages == 1..N
Clients == {"C1", "C2", "C3"}  \* Example client set

VARIABLE free, allocated

        /\ allocated = [c \in Clients |-> {}]

\* Allocate a page p to client c if p is free
Alloc(c, p) == /\ p \in free
                /\ free' = free \ {p}
                /\ allocated' = [allocated EXCEPT ![c] = @ \cup {p}]

\* Release a page p from client c
Release(c, p) == /\ p \in allocated[c]
                 /\ free' = free \cup {p}
                 /\ allocated' = [allocated EXCEPT ![c] = @ \ {p}]

Next == \E c \in Clients : \E p \in Pages :
          Alloc(c, p) \/ Release(c, p)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<free, allocated>>

TypeOK == \A p \in Pages: Cardinality({c \in Clients: p \in allocated[c]}) <= 1

VARIABLES delivered, msgSeqs, subs


(* Types *)

    \/ subs : [Topic |-> SUBSET(SubIds)]
       /\\ msgSeqs : [Topic |-> Seq(Message)]          (* ordered list of messages yet undelivered *)
       /\\ delivered :
            [SubscriberId |
                [MessageId ->
                    BOOLEAN]]                         (* true iff this sub got that Msg *)

MsgRec == <<msgid: Nat,
           pubid: PubIds,
           payload: STRING>>

Init ==
    /\ subs = [{t \in Topics} t \mapsto {}]
    /\ msgSeqs = [{t \in Topics} t \mapsto []]     \* empty sequences
    /\ delivered =
        {sub \in SubIds |
             sub \mapsto {[m.msgid \in 1..0]: FALSE}}   \* no message ids known initially

Register(sub, topic) ==
    /\ sub \in SubIds
    /\ topic \in Topics
    /\ UNCHANGED <<pubids>>
    /\ subs' = [subs EXCEPT ![topic] = @ UNION {sub}]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<msgseqs,delivered>>  

Post(pub, topic, payld) ==
    /\ pub \in PubIds
    /\ topic \in Topics
    /\ let newID = IF Len(msgSeqs(topic))=0 THEN 1 ELSE (Last(msgSeqs(topic)).msgid)+1 in 
         \/ msgSeqs' = [msgSeqs EXCEPT ![topic]=Append(@,{<<newID,pub,payld>>}) ]
            /\\ subs' = subs          (* unchanged *)
            /\\ delivered' = delivered
       /\ UNCHANGED vars

DeliverOne ==
    LET topicsWithMsgs == {t \in Topics : Len(msgSeqs(t)) > 0}
    IN  
      /\ EXISTS t \in topicsWithMsgs :
           /\ subscribers := subs[t]
           /\ msgsToSend := Head(msgSeqs[t])
           /\ FORALL s \in subscribers:
                delivered'[s][msgsToSend.msgid] = TRUE
           /\ msgSeqs' = [msgSeqs EXCEPT ![t] = Tail(@)]
        /\ Unchanged(vars)

(* Termination disjunct to avoid deadlock when no actions possible *)    
Terminate ==
     /\ UNCHANGED <<subs,msgseqs,delivered>>

Next ==
   \/ Register(sub,\*sub\*,topic)              \\ placeholder for actual parameters
   \/ Post(pub,\*pub*\ , topic, payld)
   \/ DeliverOne
   \/ Terminate

Spec == Init /\ [] ( Next )_vars

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

\* Variables:

\*   - want : [{{P1}, {P2}} -> BOOLEAN]   
\*
\* The array 'want'[p] indicates whether process p wishes to enter its critical section

    /\ turn \in {{P1},{P2}}

    /\ turn \in {{P1},{P2}}

    /\ want[P2] = FALSE

Next ==
    \/ ProcessOneEntry
    \/ ProcessTwoEntry
    \/ ProcessOneExit
    /\\ Terminating      \* allow dead‑lock free termination if desired

ProcessOneEntry == 
    /\ want' = [[w IN {P1,P2}] |-> IF w=P1 THEN TRUE ELSE want[w]]
    /\ UNCHANGED turn
    /\ (turn'=P1)

ProcessTwoEntry == 
    /\ want' = [[w IN {P1,P2}] |-> IF w=P2 THEN TRUE ELSE want[w]]    
    /\ UNCHANGEd turn
    /\ (turn'=P2) 

(* Exiting processes set their flag back to false *)
ProcessOneExit  == 
    /\ want'[P1]=FALSE
    /\ UNCHANGED [turn, want[~P1]]

ProcessTwoExit ==  
    /\ want'[P2]=FALSE
    /\ UNCHANGED [turn,want[~P2]]

Terminating ==   
   /\ UNCHANGED <<want,turn>>

Spec == Init /\ [] Next
